workflow.name: Puber Feature Delivery
workflow.description: Plan, implement, audit, fix, optionally smoke-test, and conservatively clean up a Puber Android feature.

transition_groups.description: Start Puber feature planning from the task body.
Plan is ready; continue to implementation automatically when no ambiguity remains.
plan cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
More implementation steps remain; continue in the same session.
All implementation steps are complete; prepare parallel read-only verification.
implement cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
Fixes are complete; rerun parallel read-only verification.
fix cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
Work passed this stage; run mandatory compliance review before any PR/cleanup path.
Smoke test found implementation issues; continue to fixes without manual approval.
smoke cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
Cleanup report is ready; finish the task.
Compliance passed; create or update the task PR before any terminal completion.
Compliance review found violations; continue to fixes without manual approval.
compliance cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
PR exists; monitor GitHub checks when available.
No PR is applicable because there are no repository changes or the task is explicitly report-only; user approval is required before cleanup without a merged PR.
ship_pr cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
PR checks passed or were intentionally skipped; wait until the PR is merged before cleanup/done.
PR checks failed due to task changes; continue to fixes without manual approval.
ci_monitor cannot safely continue until the user or external system resolves the blocker; remain in the same stage.
PR creation/update hit a recoverable branch, rebase, conflict, or metadata issue; user approval required before recovery work.
PR is confirmed merged; cleanup can finish the task.
PR review, conflicts, or post-CI regressions need task-scoped fixes.
PR is still waiting on a human or external process; remain in Waiting PR.
User explicitly closed/canceled the PR without merge; cleanup can finish.
Latest user comment explicitly cancels this task or declares it not planned; finish without treating it as a recoverable blocker.
Run read-only audit and deterministic compilation in parallel.
Read-only audit completed with one normalized outcome and report.
Deterministic compilation completed and reported its status.
Both direct fan-out branches completed; evaluate their stable reports.
Audit or deterministic compilation requires task-scoped changes.
Both checks passed and focused device smoke testing is useful.
Both checks passed and no device smoke test is needed.
Verification is blocked; after approval rerun both direct verification branches with fresh context.

edges.prompt_template:
Run the Puber feature planning flow for this Kent task in one Plan session.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first, then use .kent/commands/feature-start.md as the project feature planning procedure. Treat the task title/body as the feature arguments and source context. Resolve or create an explicit .todo/<feature> workspace; do not infer or create .todo/.current.

Keep bootstrap, optional design ingestion, spec creation, and implementation planning in this session. Read feature-design.md, feature-spec.md, and feature-plan.md as procedure modules when needed, but do not invoke nested /prompt:* flows or start child sessions for those phases.

Gather available Figma/spec/source context through project-local adapters only when present. Produce .todo/<feature>/spec.md as needed and .todo/<feature>/plan.md. Do not implement production code.

Complete with transition implement when the plan is ready and has no unresolved product/API/UX ambiguity and provide workspace_path plus plan_path. Complete with needs_user_action if required source context or product input is unavailable, and provide blocker_reason in the task language when practical.

Implement the Puber feature plan.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use .kent/commands/feature-implement.md with explicit workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and plan {{.Params.plan_path}}. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Implement the next unchecked ready step. Work sequentially and complete one plan step per workflow iteration. Use kent run --agent=implementation-worker for a bounded implementation slice only when delegation materially helps, and use kent run --agent=build-doctor for noisy compile failures.

Verify with the narrowest relevant Gradle/test command, using ./tools/agentw in .kent/worktrees and ./gradlew in the main checkout. Update plan.md progress; do not mirror lifecycle or step progress into meta.json.

If more unchecked implementation steps remain, complete with transition continue_implementation and provide workspace_path plus plan_path again. If all implementation steps are complete, complete with transition verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the plan path and implementation verification. Complete with needs_user_action on unresolved blockers and provide blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path to the plan.md used by this workflow transition.

edges.prompt_template:
Resume this Puber workflow stage after a user-visible blocker was resolved or explicit permission was granted.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, the latest task comments, and the previous blocker reason: {{.Params.blocker_reason}}.

Do not restart from backlog, do not discard existing .todo/worktree/PR context, and do not broaden scope. Continue only within the current stage. If the blocker is still present, add or update a task comment with the exact next action, then complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason again. Never use a terminal state for a recoverable blocker. If the task was explicitly canceled, use a close/no-PR path only when that transition exists and the latest user comment clearly asks for it.

Approval by itself is only permission to re-check this stage; it is not proof that the blocker was resolved. Before taking any non-wait transition, verify a new human task comment, task body update, or external proof that specifically resolves the previous blocker. If that proof is absent or the blocker still exists, add/update a task comment with the exact next action and complete with needs_user_action again, providing blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Short human-readable blocker explanation in the task language when practical; include the missing access/input/check and the exact next user action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Continue the same Puber feature implementation.

Read .kent/project-contract.md, then use .kent/commands/feature-implement.md with explicit workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and plan {{.Params.plan_path}}. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Select the next unchecked ready step, implement it, verify it, and update plan.md. Do not mirror lifecycle or step progress into meta.json. Use project-local delegated agents only for bounded work: implementation-worker and build-doctor.

Complete with continue_implementation and provide workspace_path plus plan_path if more steps remain. Complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the plan path and implementation verification when all implementation steps are complete. Complete with needs_user_action and blocker_reason if needs_user_action.

For any needs_user_action transition, provide blocker_reason with the exact next user or external action needed.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path to the plan.md used by this workflow transition.
Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Plan, implementation, fix, or blocker context needed by both verification branches.

edges.prompt_template:
Run mandatory Puber Compliance Review before cleanup.

Read .kent/commands/compliance-review.md, .kent/project-contract.md, and AGENTS.md first. Reviewed scope: completed feature implementation in workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} after audit/smoke context {{.Params.audit_report}}. Review only compliance with task requirements, plan/design/specs, AGENTS.md, and workflow contract. Do not edit files.

Complete with ship_pr and provide compliance_report when no compliance violations are found. Complete with needs_changes and provide workspace_path plus compliance_report when fixes are required. Complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason if required sources are missing or contradictory.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path or summary of the current audit/review report.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix issues found during Puber smoke testing.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}, audit report {{.Params.audit_report}}, and smoke report {{.Params.smoke_report}}. Keep fixes narrow, verify locally, update the audit report, then complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the smoke findings, fixes, and relevant report paths. Complete with needs_user_action and blocker_reason if needs_user_action.

For any needs_user_action transition, provide blocker_reason with the exact next user or external action needed.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path or summary of the current audit/review report.
Device smoke report or artifact path.

parameters.description: Short human-readable blocker explanation in the task language when practical; include the missing access/input/check and the exact next user action needed.
Short human-readable cleanup result. Include removed/skipped/safe-to-remove worktree and branch names, or the reason cleanup could not run.

edges.prompt_template:
Create or update the Puber pull request for this completed task.

Read .kent/project-contract.md, .kent/commands/ship-pr.md, and AGENTS.md first. Compliance result: {{.Params.compliance_report}}.

If this task produced repository changes, commit only task-related files, push the current task branch to origin, and create or update a GitHub PR. Do not merge the PR. Do not push directly to master/main. If there are no repository changes and the task is report-only or smoke-only, complete with no_pr and provide pr_report explaining why PR is not applicable.

Complete with monitor_ci when a PR exists and provide pr_url, branch_name, and workspace_path. Complete with needs_changes when PR creation/update is needs_user_action by recoverable repository, branch, or PR state such as merge conflicts, non-fast-forward push, branch needing rebase, or missing PR metadata that can be fixed in the task worktree; provide workspace_path and blocker_reason. Complete with no_pr only for PR-not-applicable/no-diff cases and provide pr_report. Complete with needs_user_action only when continuing would be unsafe without changing task scope, credentials, or project policy; provide blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Compliance review report or summary.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix Puber feature compliance violations only.

Read .kent/project-contract.md and .kent/commands/compliance-review.md first. Apply only the minimum changes required by compliance findings in {{.Params.compliance_report}} for workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Verify narrowly, then complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the compliance findings, fixes, and relevant report paths. Complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason if unsafe or missing input.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Compliance review report or summary.

edges.prompt_template:
Resume this Puber workflow stage after a user-visible blocker was resolved or explicit permission was granted.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, the latest task comments, and the previous blocker reason: {{.Params.blocker_reason}}.

Do not restart from backlog, do not discard existing .todo/worktree/PR context, and do not broaden scope. Continue only within the current stage. If the blocker is still present, add or update a task comment with the exact next action, then complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason again. Approval by itself is only permission to re-check this stage; it is not proof that the blocker was resolved. Before taking any non-wait transition, verify a new human task comment, task body update, or external proof that specifically resolves the previous blocker. If that proof is absent or the blocker still exists, add/update a task comment with the exact next action and complete with needs_user_action again, providing blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Short human-readable blocker explanation in the task language when practical; include the missing access/input/check and the exact next user action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Monitor Puber PR checks.

Read .kent/project-contract.md and AGENTS.md first. Inspect PR {{.Params.pr_url}} on branch {{.Params.branch_name}}. Use GitHub CLI when available. Do not merge the PR. Do not push new commits from this node.

Complete with waiting_pr if CI/checks pass, are absent, or are intentionally skipped with a clear reason; provide ci_report and pr_url. Complete with needs_changes if CI failed due to task changes and provide workspace_path plus ci_report. Complete with needs_user_action for temporary external waits; provide blocker_reason. Complete with needs_user_action only if checks cannot be inspected and retrying would be unsafe or misleading; provide blocker_reason.

parameters.description: URL of the created, updated, or monitored pull request.
Name of the task or release branch associated with the pull request.
Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.

edges.prompt_template:
Run conservative Puber task cleanup after the user approved the PR-not-applicable path.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first, then use .kent/commands/cleanup-task.md. PR report: {{.Params.pr_report}}. Cleanup without a merged PR is approval-gated; do not treat absence of repository changes as permission to delete anything. Do not delete any worktree containing unpushed commits, uncommitted user changes, or unclear ownership. Complete with done and provide cleanup_report.

parameters.description: PR creation, review, conflict, or post-CI regression report.

edges.prompt_template:
Resume this Puber workflow stage after a user-visible blocker was resolved or explicit permission was granted.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, the latest task comments, and the previous blocker reason: {{.Params.blocker_reason}}.

Do not restart from backlog, do not discard existing .todo/worktree/PR context, and do not broaden scope. Continue only within the current stage. If the blocker is still present, add or update a task comment with the exact next action, then complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason again. Never use a terminal state for a recoverable blocker. If the task was explicitly canceled, use a close/no-PR path only when that transition exists and the latest user comment clearly asks for it.

Approval by itself is only permission to re-check this stage; it is not proof that the blocker was resolved. Before taking any non-wait transition, verify a new human task comment, task body update, or external proof that specifically resolves the previous blocker. If that proof is absent or the blocker still exists, add/update a task comment with the exact next action and complete with needs_user_action again, providing blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Short human-readable blocker explanation in the task language when practical; include the missing access/input/check and the exact next user action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Hold this Puber task until its pull request is actually merged.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, the latest task comments, PR {{.Params.pr_url}}, branch {{.Params.branch_name}}, workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}, and CI report {{.Params.ci_report}}.

Use `gh pr view --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit,headRefName,baseRefName,url` when available. Do not merge the PR. Do not push commits from this node. Do not clean up while the PR is still open.

Complete with pr_merged only when GitHub reports state=MERGED; provide pr_url, branch_name, and merge_report. Complete with needs_changes when PR review comments, merge conflicts, or post-CI regressions can be fixed within the task scope; provide workspace_path and pr_report. Complete with needs_user_action when the PR is still open, waiting for human review/approval/merge, or needs_user_action by an external process; add a task comment with the current PR status and exact human action, then provide pr_url, branch_name, workspace_path, and waiting_reason. Complete with close_without_merge only if the latest user comment explicitly says to close/cancel/skip this PR; provide pr_url, branch_name, and cleanup_reason.

parameters.description: URL of the created, updated, or monitored pull request.
Name of the task or release branch associated with the pull request.
Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
CI/check status report, including failures, skipped checks, or accepted absent checks.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix Puber feature CI failures only.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Apply only the minimum changes required by CI report {{.Params.ci_report}} in workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Verify narrowly, then complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the CI findings, fixes, and relevant report paths. Complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason if unsafe or missing input.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
CI/check status report, including failures, skipped checks, or accepted absent checks.

edges.prompt_template:
Resume this Puber workflow stage after a user-visible blocker was resolved or explicit permission was granted.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, the latest task comments, and the previous blocker reason: {{.Params.blocker_reason}}.

Do not restart from backlog, do not discard existing .todo/worktree/PR context, and do not broaden scope. Continue only within the current stage. If the blocker is still present, add or update a task comment with the exact next action, then complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason again. Never use a terminal state for a recoverable blocker. If the task was explicitly canceled, use a close/no-PR path only when that transition exists and the latest user comment clearly asks for it.

Approval by itself is only permission to re-check this stage; it is not proof that the blocker was resolved. Before taking any non-wait transition, verify a new human task comment, task body update, or external proof that specifically resolves the previous blocker. If that proof is absent or the blocker still exists, add/update a task comment with the exact next action and complete with needs_user_action again, providing blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Short human-readable blocker explanation in the task language when practical; include the missing access/input/check and the exact next user action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix recoverable Puber PR creation/update issues only.

Read .kent/project-contract.md, .kent/commands/ship-pr.md, and AGENTS.md first. Previous PR blocker: {{.Params.blocker_reason}}. Work in {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Apply only the minimum changes needed to make the task branch/PR shippable, such as rebasing, resolving merge conflicts, fixing branch metadata, or repairing the PR-ready commit set. Do not expand product scope. Verify narrowly, then continue through the workflow's normal review/compliance path. Complete with needs_user_action only if the recovery is unsafe or requires user credentials/policy changes.

For any needs_user_action transition, provide blocker_reason with the exact next user or external action needed.

After recovery changes, complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the PR repair and verification context.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Short human-readable blocker explanation in the task language when practical; include the missing access/input/check and the exact next user action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Run conservative Puber task cleanup after the pull request was confirmed merged.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, .kent/commands/cleanup-task.md, PR {{.Params.pr_url}}, branch {{.Params.branch_name}}, and merge report {{.Params.merge_report}}. Verify the PR is merged with GitHub state, not only git ancestry, because squash merges are allowed. Do not delete any worktree containing unpushed commits, uncommitted user changes, or unclear ownership. Complete with done and provide cleanup_report.

parameters.description: URL of the created, updated, or monitored pull request.
Name of the task or release branch associated with the pull request.
GitHub PR state, mergedAt, mergeCommit, and cleanup or release notes.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix Puber PR feedback only.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, latest task comments, workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}, and PR feedback report {{.Params.pr_report}}. Apply only task-scoped changes needed for PR review comments, merge conflicts, or post-CI regressions. Do not force-push unless the latest user comment explicitly permits force-push for this exact PR/branch. Verify narrowly, then complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the PR feedback fixes and updated report. If user input is required, complete with needs_user_action and provide blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
PR creation, review, conflict, or post-CI regression report.

edges.prompt_template:
Re-check the Puber pull request after the user resolved the external wait or approved another check.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, latest task comments, PR {{.Params.pr_url}}, branch {{.Params.branch_name}}, workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}, and previous waiting reason {{.Params.waiting_reason}}. Do not merge, push, or clean up unless the PR is confirmed merged.

Complete with pr_merged only when GitHub reports state=MERGED. Complete with needs_changes for task-scoped PR review/conflict/regression fixes. Complete with needs_user_action again if the PR is still waiting externally. Complete with close_without_merge only on explicit latest user instruction.

Approval by itself is only permission to re-check this stage; it is not proof that the blocker was resolved. Before taking any non-wait transition, verify a new human task comment, task body update, or external proof that specifically resolves the previous blocker. If that proof is absent or the PR/release PR is still waiting externally, add/update a task comment with the exact next action and complete with needs_user_action again, providing waiting_reason.

parameters.description: URL of the created, updated, or monitored pull request.
Name of the task or release branch associated with the pull request.
Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Why the PR cannot advance yet and the exact user or external action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Run conservative Puber task cleanup after the user explicitly closed/canceled the PR without merge.

Read AGENTS.md, .kent/project-contract.md, .kent/commands/cleanup-task.md, PR {{.Params.pr_url}}, branch {{.Params.branch_name}}, and cleanup reason {{.Params.cleanup_reason}}. Do not delete any worktree containing unpushed commits, uncommitted user changes, or unclear ownership. Complete with done and provide cleanup_report.

parameters.description: URL of the created, updated, or monitored pull request.
Name of the task or release branch associated with the pull request.
Explicit user instruction or reason for cleanup without merge.
Explicit latest user instruction explaining why this task should be closed as not planned or canceled.

edges.prompt_template:
Run the read-only Puber feature audit in parallel with deterministic compilation.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Workspace: {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Review context: {{.Params.review_context}}. Use .kent/commands/feature-audit.md, but do not run Gradle because the sibling Compile Verify branch is authoritative for compilation. Do not edit files. Delegate bounded read-only review to quality-reviewer, compose-reviewer, or domain-model-reviewer only when relevant.

Write or update the audit report under the feature workspace. Complete only with transition reported and provide audit_outcome plus audit_result. audit_outcome must be exactly one of needs_changes, smoke, compliance, or needs_user_action. audit_result must include the report path or summary and the findings, smoke rationale, or exact blocker for that outcome.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Plan, implementation, fix, or blocker context needed by both verification branches.
Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Plan, implementation, fix, or blocker context needed by both verification branches.
Audit outcome: needs_changes, smoke, compliance, or needs_user_action.
Audit report package containing report path or summary and the reason, findings, or blocker.
Deterministic compile outcome: passed or failed.
Compile command and log path produced by deterministic verification.

edges.prompt_template:
Apply only the fixes required by the joined Puber verification results.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Workspace: {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Audit: {{.Params.audit_report}}. Compile: {{.Params.verification_report}}. Keep changes narrow and single-writer. Use implementation-worker for bounded fixes and build-doctor for diagnostics when useful.

After fixes, complete with verify and provide workspace_path plus review_context summarizing the findings, fixes, and relevant report paths. Complete with needs_user_action and blocker_reason only when continuing is unsafe without user or external input.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path or summary of the current audit/review report.
Compile command and log path produced by deterministic verification.

edges.prompt_template:
Run a focused Puber device smoke test.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use .kent/commands/smoke-test.md for workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and audit report {{.Params.audit_report}}. Deterministic compile report: {{.Params.verification_report}}. Follow the emulator resource-lock and explicit adb serial rules in AGENTS.md. Build and install a fresh devDebug APK before testing.

Complete with compliance if smoke passes. Complete with needs_changes if smoke finds implementation issues and provide workspace_path, audit_report, and smoke_report. Complete with needs_user_action and blocker_reason for unresolved external blockers.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path or summary of the current audit/review report.
Compile command and log path produced by deterministic verification.

edges.prompt_template:
Run mandatory Puber Compliance Review before PR or cleanup.

Read .kent/commands/compliance-review.md, .kent/project-contract.md, and AGENTS.md first. Workspace: {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Audit: {{.Params.audit_report}}. Deterministic compile: {{.Params.verification_report}}. Review only compliance; do not edit files.

Complete with ship_pr and compliance_report when compliant. Complete with needs_changes and provide workspace_path plus compliance_report when fixes are required. Complete with needs_user_action and blocker_reason if required sources are missing or contradictory.

parameters.description: Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Path or summary of the current audit/review report.
Compile command and log path produced by deterministic verification.
Path to the task workspace or worktree relevant to this workflow transition.
Plan, implementation, fix, or blocker context needed by both verification branches.
Short human-readable blocker explanation and exact next user or external action needed.
